diagrams.title: Agile Manifesto 4 Values
diagrams.description: Shows the four key values of the Agile Manifesto in contrast pairs
elements: Individuals and interactions > Processes and tools
Responding to change > Following a plan
diagrams.purpose: To memorize the core philosophy of Agile methodology

diagrams.title: Agile vs Waterfall Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of how Waterfall flows sequentially through phases vs Agile's iterative cycles
elements: Waterfall: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Verification → Maintenance (linear, one direction)
Agile: Plan → Develop → Test → Deploy → Review → (repeat with feedback)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental difference between methodologies

diagrams.title: Scrum Framework Overview
diagrams.description: Complete Scrum framework showing roles, events, and artifacts in a Sprint cycle
elements: Product Backlog → Sprint Planning → Sprint Backlog → Daily Scrum → Development → Sprint Review → Increment → Sprint Retrospective → Product Backlog
Roles shown around: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Development Team
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how Scrum organizes Agile practice

diagrams.title: Sprint Cycle Flow
diagrams.description: Detailed flow of activities within a single Sprint
elements: Day 14: Sprint Review (4 hrs max), Sprint Retrospective (3 hrs max)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding time-boxed events within a Sprint

visuals.title: What Is An Agile Process
visuals.description: Visual diagram for what is an agile process
